decision.summary: Proceed with owner-hierarchy structural redesign after Stage31 Coverage/Entailment diagnostics.
decision.denied_form: Mamba Encoder -> multiple heads -> learned black-box arbiter -> final label
decision.approved_form: Mamba Encoder -> explicit owners -> rule-structured composer -> final label

denied_paths: learned all-head mixing as primary final decision

stage31_summary.conclusion: Stage31-D composer integration is denied; Coverage/Entailment must be promoted to a first-class owner/layer.

evidence.interpretation: Conservative but under-structured; suppresses many overclaims but fails to preserve valid SUPPORT entailment.
Partial signal exists but unsafe for composer integration.
Hard contrast did not solve the problem; overclaim ownership collapsed.
Raw pair access does not make the neural diagnostic head stable enough.
Hybrid access does not make the neural diagnostic head stable enough.

architecture.text_diagram: Mamba Encoder -> Hard Core Validity Owner -> Coverage / Entailment Owner -> Residual Adjudication Owner -> ANI Diagnostic Readout -> Polarity Owner -> Rule-Structured Composer -> Final Label
principles: Hard Core failures cannot be rescued
Residual Adjudication is not cap stacking
ANI is diagnostic, not primary decision logic

owners.input: Encoded claim/evidence target, event, relation, entity, location, and temporal anchors
owners.authority: Hard failure cannot be rescued by later owners; may block SUPPORT/REFUTE when target identity or event anchor is broken.
failure_mode_owned: wrong location anchor when location is core
wrong temporal anchor when time is core

owners.input: Hard-Core-valid pair state plus direct directional scope/coverage features
owners.authority: Overclaim routes to NOT_ENTITLED unless a stronger contradiction applies; entailment-preserving relation preserves entitlement; contradiction routes toward REFUTE.

owners.input: Cases not cleanly resolved by Hard Core or Coverage/Entailment
owners.authority: Produces residual state for ANI and composer; should not override Hard Core or straightforward Coverage/Entailment.

owners.input: Owner states from Hard Core, Coverage/Entailment, Residual, and Polarity
owners.authority: Diagnostic explanation layer only; not primary final-label controller.
failure_mode_owned: ignorance from insufficient entitlement or missing coverage
novelty from information not licensed by evidence

owners.authority: Decides SUPPORT vs REFUTE only after entitlement is alive or contradiction is explicitly licensed; cannot rescue failed Hard Core or turn overclaim into SUPPORT.
failure_mode_owned: explicit support/refute polarity after ownership routing

owners.input: All owner states and calibrated owner confidences
owners.authority: Applies explicit owner priority and authority rules; replaces black-box final MLP arbitration.

composer_rules: Hard Core failure blocks unsupported final SUPPORT and cannot be rescued by later owners.
If Hard Core passes and Coverage/Entailment identifies overclaim, route to NOT_ENTITLED unless a stronger contradiction owner explicitly applies.
If Hard Core passes and Coverage/Entailment identifies entailment-preserving relation, preserve the SUPPORT entitlement path and prevent unnecessary NOT_ENTITLED collapse.
If Coverage/Entailment identifies contradiction, route toward REFUTE through polarity/contradiction handling.
Residual unresolved cases route to NOT_ENTITLED or ANI-informed uncertainty without overriding Hard Core or clear Coverage/Entailment.
Polarity decides SUPPORT/REFUTE only after entitlement is established or contradiction is explicitly licensed.
Composer may use calibrated owner confidence, but owner priority and authority must be explicit.
No black-box final MLP arbiter and no learned all-head mixing as primary final decision.

implementation_roadmap.goal: Introduce owner state dataclass / structured output schema without changing final predictions.
Implement Hard Core + Coverage/Entailment owner interfaces.
Implement rule-structured composer in shadow mode.
Evaluate shadow composer on Stage31 probe and controlled dev.
Only then consider replacing v7 final decision.

risks: Stage31 probe is templated and diagnostic, not a broad benchmark.
Explicit owner hierarchy may initially reduce aggregate dev accuracy.
Need to avoid training leakage from Stage31 probe.
